title: 10 best herbal capsules for palpitation
intro_desc: Herbal capsules for palpitation are dietary supplements that contain a combination of herbs and other natural ingredients, which can help regulate heartbeat and reduce symptoms of palpitations. These herbs, such as Hawthorn, Motherwort, and Wort, have been used in traditional medicine for centuries to treat heart-related conditions. They work by improving blood flow, reducing anxiety, and strengthening the cardiovascular system. By taking herbal capsules for palpitation, individuals can experience a decrease in the frequency and intensity of palpitations, leading to improved quality of life. For example, a person who previously experienced frequent and distressing palpitations may find that, after taking herbal capsules, they are able to engage in daily activities without the interruption or worry of heart palpitations.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Motherwort capsule helps with palpitation because it contains active compounds like alkaloids, flavonoids, and iridoid glycosides, which have a positive effect on the heart. These compounds help to regulate heartbeat, reduce palpitations, and promote a steady heart rhythm. Additionally, motherwort has been shown to have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help to reduce anxiety and stress, both of which can contribute to heart palpitations. By taking motherwort capsules, individuals may find relief from symptoms of palpitations and experience a greater sense of calm and well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-arrhythmic: Motherwort contains alkaloids such as leonurine, which helps regulate heartbeat and reduce palpitations by suppressing abnormal heart rhythms.
Anxiolytic: Motherwort has been shown to have a calming effect on the nervous system due to its content of iridoids and flavonoids, reducing anxiety and stress-induced palpitations.
Vasodilatory: Motherwort's active constituents, including essential oils and tannins, help dilate blood vessels, improving circulation and reducing the intensity of palpitations.

Antioxidant: The presence of phenolic acids and flavonoids in motherwort provides antioxidant properties, helping protect heart tissues from oxidative damage and maintaining overall cardiovascular health.

Cardioprotective: Motherwort has been found to have a protective effect on heart muscle cells, reducing damage from ischemia-reperfusion injury and aiding in the prevention of palpitations caused by heart-related conditions.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the motherwort plant are commonly used to make medicinal capsules for palpitation due to their calming and anti-arrhythmic properties.
Flowers: The flowers of the motherwort plant are used in making medicinal capsules for palpitation because they contain active compounds that help regulate heart rhythm.
Seeds: The seeds of the motherwort plant are used in making medicinal capsules for palpitation as they are rich in antioxidants and alkaloids that support heart health.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2-3 tablespoons of dried motherwort herb, and grind it into a fine powder.
Mix the motherwort powder with 0.5-1 teaspoon of a binding agent, such as gelatin or plant-based gum, to help the capsule hold its shape.
Fill size "00" empty gelatin or vegetarian capsules with the motherwort-binding agent mixture, using a capsule-filling machine or by hand.
Allow the filled capsules to dry for 24 hours in a cool, dark place to ensure the binding agent sets properly.
Store the finished motherwort capsules in an airtight container, in a cool, dark place, for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian capsule helps with palpitation because it contains valerian root, a natural substance known for its calming and relaxing properties. This herb has been used for centuries to treat various ailments, including heart-related issues. Valerian is believed to help regulate heartbeat by increasing the availability of a chemical called gamma-aminobutyric acid (GABA) in the brain. GABA has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help reduce the severity and frequency of palpitations. Additionally, valerian is thought to have a mild sedative effect, which can further help to alleviate symptoms of palpitation by promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic: Valerian capsules can help reduce anxiety, which is important for palpitation because anxiety and stress can trigger or worsen heart palpitations. The active constituents responsible for this property include valerenic acid, valerenol, and sesquiterpene.

Sedative: Valerian capsules have a sedative effect, which can help alleviate symptoms of palpitation by promoting relaxation and reducing muscle tension. The active constituents responsible for this property include valepotriates and isovaltrate.

Hypnotic: Valerian capsules can help improve sleep quality, which is important for palpitation because poor sleep can contribute to the development and worsening of heart palpitations. The active constituents responsible for this property include valepotriates and isovaltrate.

Antispasmodic: Valerian capsules can help reduce muscle spasms, which is important for palpitation because spasms in the heart or surrounding muscles can cause or worsen heart palpitations. The active constituents responsible for this property include valerenic acid and valtrates.

Cardioprotective: Valerian capsules have been shown to have a protective effect on the heart, which is important for palpitation because it can help reduce the risk of developing heart-related conditions that can cause or worsen palpitations. The active constituents responsible for this property include flavonoids, alkaloids, and tannins.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of valerian plant are commonly used in making medicinal capsules for palpitation due to their sedative and calming properties.
Rhizomes: Valerian rhizomes are used for medicinal purposes as they contain active compounds that help regulate heartbeat and reduce symptoms of palpitation.
Flowers: Valerian flowers are used in making medicinal capsules for palpitation as they contain volatile oils that have a calming effect on the nervous system.
Seeds: Valerian seeds are used in medicinal capsules for palpitation due to their high concentration of essential oils that have a relaxing effect on the body.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 teaspoons of dried valerian root and grind it into a fine powder, which may take 5-10 minutes.
Combine the valerian root powder with 1 teaspoon of tapioca starch or rice flour as a filler, mixing thoroughly.
Divide the powder mixture evenly into 10-12 empty gelatin capsules, using a capsule filling machine or manually.
Store the completed valerian capsules in an airtight container in a cool, dark place for up to 6 months.
Take one or two capsules as needed, 30 minutes before bedtime, or as directed by a healthcare professional.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop capsule helps with palpitation because it contains passionflower, which is known for its calming and relaxing properties. This herb may help reduce symptoms of anxiety and stress, which can often trigger heart palpitations. Maypop can help promote relaxation and improve sleep quality, which can also contribute to a normal heart rhythm. Additionally, maypop has antioxidant properties that may help protect the heart and cardiovascular system from damage. By reducing anxiety and promoting relaxation, maypop capsules can help alleviate heart palpitations and promote overall cardiovascular health.
remedy_properties: Anti-arrhythmic: Maypop contains alkaloids such as chrysin and coreopsin, which help regulate heartbeat and reduce irregular heart rhythms, thus alleviating palpitations.
Anxiolytic: Maypop has been shown to have anxiety-reducing effects due to its flavonoid content, which can help manage palpitations caused by anxiety or stress.
Sedative: The plant contains sedative properties, primarily from its alkaloids and flavonoids, which can help reduce palpitations by calming the nervous system.
Vasodilatory: Maypop has vasodilatory effects due to its alkaloid and flavonoid content, which can help lower blood pressure and reduce palpitations caused by hypertension.
Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of maypop, attributed to its flavonoid content, can help protect the heart from oxidative stress and inflammation, thereby alleviating palpitations.
remedy_parts: Roots: The maypop roots are used in making medicinal capsules for palpitation due to their sedative properties that help regulate heartbeat.
Leaves: Maypop leaves are used in medicinal capsules for palpitation because they contain antispasmodic compounds that help soothe heart muscle spasms.
Seeds: The seeds of the maypop plant are used in medicinal capsules for palpitation due to their high concentration of flavonoids, which have been shown to have a calming effect on the heart.
Flowers: Maypop flowers are used in medicinal capsules for palpitation because they contain anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that can help alleviate heart-related discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 tablespoons of dried maypop (Passiflora incarnata) leaves and let them air dry for 24 hours.
Grind the dried maypop leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Measure out 500mg of the maypop powder and fill size 00 gelatin capsules using a capsule filling machine.
Store the completed maypop capsules in an airtight container in a cool, dry place for up to 6 months.
Take 1-2 maypop capsules, as needed, to help manage palpitations. Consult with a healthcare professional before starting any new supplement regimen.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: European mistletoe capsule helps with palpitation because it contains active compounds that may help regulate heart function. Mistletoe has been found to have a positive inotropic effect, meaning it can improve the contraction of heart muscles, which can help regulate heartbeat and reduce palpitations. Additionally, mistletoe extracts have been shown to have a mild sedative effect, which can help calm the body and reduce anxiety, another common cause of palpitations. However, it's important to note that mistletoe should only be taken under the guidance of a healthcare professional, as it can interact with certain medications and may cause side effects in some individuals.
remedy_properties: Antiarrhythmic property: European mistletoe helps regulate heartbeat and reduce palpitations by containing cardiac glycosides, such as viscoside and convallatoxin, which help maintain a normal heart rhythm.

Vasodilatory property: Mistletoe extract can widen blood vessels, improving blood flow and reducing the workload on the heart, which is beneficial for people experiencing palpitations due to poor circulation or hypertension, thanks to its flavonoids and triterpene content.

Anxiolytic property: European mistletoe has calming and anxiety-reducing effects, which can help alleviate palpitations triggered by stress, panic attacks, or anxiety disorders, due to the presence of alkaloids like viscotine and viscin.

Antioxidant property: Mistletoe extract contains various antioxidants that protect heart cells from oxidative damage and inflammation, which can contribute to a healthier cardiovascular system and reduced palpitations, owing to its high content of phenolic acids, flavonoids, and other polyphenols.

Immunomodulatory property: European mistletoe can help regulate and balance the immune system, which can be beneficial for people with autoimmune disorders or chronic inflammation that might lead to palpitations. This is attributed to the lectins and viscotoxins found in mistletoe.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the European mistletoe plant are commonly used to make medicinal capsules for palpitation due to their high concentration of active compounds, such as viscotoxins and alkaloids, which have been shown to have a regulatory effect on heart function.

Berries: Although not typically used in medicinal capsules, the berries of the European mistletoe plant contain active compounds that can have a positive effect on heart function and may be used in some forms of traditional medicine.

Stems: The stems of the European mistletoe plant contain a range of active compounds, including viscotoxins and alkaloids, which can have a positive effect on heart function and may be used in the production of medicinal capsules for palpitation.

Bark: The bark of the European mistletoe plant is rich in active compounds, including viscotoxins and alkaloids, which have been shown to have a regulatory effect on heart function and may be used in the production of medicinal capsules for palpitation.
remedy_recipe: Gather fresh European mistletoe (Viscum album) branches, ensuring they are harvested sustainably and legally.
Thoroughly clean the mistletoe, discarding any damaged or discolored parts, then allow it to air dry for 3-5 days.
Finely chop the dried mistletoe into small pieces, then evenly mix 28 grams of the herb with 42 grams of a non-allergenic filler, such as tapioca starch or rice flour.
Fill size "00" vegetarian capsules with the mistletoe-filler mixture, using a capsule filling machine or manually.
Seal the capsules and store in airtight containers, preferably in a cool, dark location, for up to 6 months. Consult a healthcare professional before consuming for palpitation treatment.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lily of the valley capsule helps with palpitation because it contains natural glycosides, such as convallotoxin, which can help regulate the heartbeat and improve cardiac function. These glycosides work by increasing the force of heart contractions and slowing down the heart rate, which can help alleviate symptoms of palpitation. Additionally, the herb has been shown to have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help reduce anxiety and stress, further helping to regulate heart rhythm. Overall, lily of the valley capsule can be a natural and effective way to manage palpitations and support heart health.
remedy_properties: Cardiac glycosides content: Lily of the valley capsules contain cardiac glycosides, such as convallatoxin and convallamarin, which help strengthen heart contractions and regulate heart rate, thus alleviating palpitations.
Anti-arrhythmic properties: The plant extract has been found to exhibit anti-arrhythmic effects, which help restore and maintain a normal heart rhythm, reducing irregular heartbeats and palpitations.

Vasodilatory effects: Lily of the valley contains compounds that dilate blood vessels, improving blood flow and oxygen supply to the heart, thereby reducing palpitations caused by poor cardiac oxygenation.

Diuretic properties: Lily of the valley has diuretic properties due to the presence of cardiac glycosides and saponins, which help eliminate excess fluid and reduce the workload on the heart, alleviating palpitations.

Nervine and sedative effects: The plant contains compounds with nervine and sedative properties, which can help reduce anxiety and stress, both of which can contribute to palpitations, by promoting relaxation and calmness.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Contain cardiac glycosides and saponins, which help regulate heartbeat and reduce palpitations.
Rhizomes: Rich in glycosides and alkaloids, they are used to treat various heart-related conditions, including palpitations.
Leaves: Contain anti-arrhythmic compounds, which help regulate heart rhythm and alleviate palpitations.
Roots: Have cardiotonic properties due to the presence of glycosides, making them useful for managing palpitations.
Buds: Contain high levels of active compounds, such as glycosides, which help regulate heart function and reduce palpitations.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 tablespoons of fresh or dried lily of the valley flowers and leaves, and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the lily of the valley in the boiling water for 10-15 minutes to create a strong herbal infusion.
Strain the liquid, pressing out the plant material to extract all the beneficial compounds.
Combine the liquid with 0.5-1 teaspoon of a natural binder, such as arrowroot powder or psyllium husk, to help form the capsules.
Fill size 00 gelatin or vegetarian capsules with the herbal mixture, using a capsule filling machine or by hand. Allow the capsules to dry completely before storing in a tightly sealed container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Foxglove capsule helps with palpitation because it contains digitalis, a substance that has a positive inotropic effect on the heart, meaning it strengthens the contraction of the heart muscle. This, in turn, can help regulate heart rhythms and reduce palpitations. Additionally, foxglove has been shown to slow the conduction of electrical signals in the heart, which can also help to stabilize heart rhythms. However, it's important to note that foxglove should only be taken under the supervision of a healthcare professional, as it can have serious side effects if not used correctly.
remedy_properties: Cardiotonic: Foxglove capsule contains cardiac glycosides such as digitoxin and digoxin, which increase the force of heart contractions and help regulate heart rate, making it effective in treating palpitations.
Antiarrhythmic: The cardiac glycosides in foxglove capsule can help stabilize the heart's rhythm, reducing irregular heartbeats and palpitations.
Positive inotropic: By increasing the contractility of heart muscles, the active constituents in foxglove capsule can help improve overall heart function and alleviate symptoms of palpitations.
Negative chronotropic: The cardiac glycosides in foxglove capsule can slow down the heart rate, which is beneficial for individuals experiencing palpitations due to a rapid heart rhythm.
Vasodilatory: The active constituents in foxglove capsule can widen blood vessels, reducing the workload on the heart and alleviating symptoms of palpitations.
